input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges` isn't the right property here. You need two things:

**1. iOS — disable safe area insets on the page:**
```xml
xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;
             assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"
```

**2. Hide any navigation chrome** (if using Shell or NavigationPage):
```xml
Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
<!-- or -->
NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"
```

**Full example:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False">

    <Image Source="photo.jpg"
           Aspect="AspectFill"
           VerticalOptions="Fill"
           HorizontalOptions="Fill" />
</ContentPage>
```

**On Android (.NET 10):** Edge-to-edge is enforced by default on Android 15+, so your content already draws behind system bars. If you're seeing gaps on older Android versions, make the status/nav bars transparent in your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
Window.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
Window.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
```

The key takeaway: `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` is the iOS equivalent of what you were trying to do, and hiding the Shell/NavigationPage bar removes the top toolbar padding.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about making content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but the image still has padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what they need:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve true edge-to-edge content that goes behind system bars, you need to:

1. Set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the view (the Image itself), not just on the ContentPage
2. On Android, you may need to enable edge-to-edge mode


3. The `SafeAreaEdges` property on ContentPage controls which edges respect the safe area, but individual views also need `IgnoreSafeArea` set. On iOS specifically, you might need platform-specific configuration to disable safe area handling, while Android requires the window to be configured to draw behind system bars.

Actually, I'm realizing `SafeAreaEdges` isn't a standard MAUI property—the real solution is using `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the views themselves, or `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` at the page level. For Android edge-to-edge in .NET 9+, it's often the default behavior, though window inset handling might still be needed depending on the setup. status bar / navigation bar to transparent

The user mentioned `SafeAreaEdges="None"`, which isn't quite right—they probably need `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"` instead. I should clarify the correct approach: on iOS, set that property and use `Aspect="AspectFill"` on the Image, while on Android I might need to configure edge-to-edge behavior. If they're using Shell or NavigationPage, I can hide the nav bar with `Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"` or `NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"`. There's also `IgnoreSafeArea` on View elements that could help.

For .NET 10, edge-to-edge is actually the default on Android now (that changed starting in .NET 9 for Android 15+), so I should keep that in mind when giving the solution.
